labels.description: The artifact or edge is declared and can be checked for existence only.
The artifact bytes are checked against a recorded digest.
The edge was observed from a declared pin, package coordinate, copied literal, or repo-relative source reference.

Attested by a byte-reproducible parity certificate: the artifact's rendered bytes fail-closed re-verify against a digest bind to the certified desired-state graph S (C1-C5 cert bind passed). Proves BYTE PARITY with certified truth. Does NOT prove a human or authority ratified it — no authority event is required or checked for this tier. This is exactly what CS proj-S certification (spec #74, portal #165) ships today. Supersedes the bare 'certified' id (CS-1.4 label rescope, docs/design/CS-1.4-certified-label-rescope.md).

Everything parity-certified proves, PLUS a valid, signature-verified authority event (authority_event_protocol.v1 core/chain split, Ed25519) bound to the same cert_digest, ratified by a human through the XG-1 authority path. Proves BYTE PARITY *and* AUTHORITY RATIFICATION. Reserved: no artifact earns this label until the XG-1 authority loop (contract Slices 1-4; DESIGN-xg1-completion.md §4) lands end-to-end and delivers a verified authority-event.json alongside the cert. The higher of the two certified tiers (CS-1.4 label rescope).

DEPRECATED (CS-1.4 label rescope). The bare 'certified' id was ambiguous about whether it meant byte-parity or authority ratification; in practice every artifact that has ever carried it had parity only, never a verified authority event. Alias of parity-certified — NEVER authority-certified, since aliasing an unratified artifact upward would manufacture the exact false-green this rescope exists to close. Retained so producers/consumers already emitting the bare 'certified' id keep validating; new artifacts MUST emit parity-certified or authority-certified. Do not use in new manifests.
The artifact is declared in the governed set and is present, but does not conform to its declared class or contract shape.
A present artifact that is not declared in the governed set (governed:false). Ingestion must never treat it as authoritative or governed; it is neither owed nor checked.
A cross-repo edge whose counterpart repo has not adopted the contract (governed:false at the far end). The edge may be observed locally, but the far end is ungoverned and carries no adoption profile.

label_kinds.evidence: Evidence-strength axis: how strongly the artifact/edge is attested (presence -> hash -> realized -> parity-certified -> authority-certified). CS-1.4 splits the former single 'certified' rung into two honest tiers: parity-certified (byte parity, no authority event) and authority-certified (parity PLUS a verified, human-ratified authority event) — see each entry's description. The bare 'certified' id is kept only as a deprecated alias of parity-certified; it is never an alias of authority-certified.
label_kinds.governance: Governance-status axis: whether and how an artifact/edge is governed. Orthogonal to evidence strength.
